channel.title: Berlin Daily
channel.description: Your daily dose of Berlin news.

item.title: 🏗️ Easier construction, Gas shortage, Hantavirus - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The Berlin Senate approved the "Einfacher-Bauen-Gesetz" to accelerate residential construction. To make converting existing properties into housing easier, the legislation lowers technical standards for fire and sound protection and reduces minimum ceiling heights for attics. For new developments, the law exempts builders from including storage rooms. Construction senator Christian Gaebler expects the parliament to pass the bill before the summer break. (rbb24)

item.title: 🚗 Car-free vote, TU closure, Grid sabotage - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The “Berlin autofrei” initiative failed to secure the 175,000 signatures needed to advance its proposal to ban most private cars inside Berlin’s S-Bahn ring. Activists collected approximately 140,000 signatures before the May deadline, falling short of the threshold required to trigger a citywide referendum. (Spiegel)

item.title: 🚋 50-meter trams, Scooter ban, Alexa+ - Berlin News Daily
item.description: BVG plans to launch its new 50-meter Urbanliner trams this summer after abruptly canceling the February premiere. BVG chief Henrik Falk admitted that poor coordination with the technical supervisory authority caused the sudden delay. Planners initially underestimated the structural requirements needed to handle the massive vehicles over the aging Alexanderplatz subway station. (rbb24)

item.title: 🏠 Rent registry, Suspected pedophiles, Anti-semitism grants - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin's CDU/SPD coalition plans to introduce a mandatory "rent registry" to combat illegal rent hikes ahead of upcoming city elections. Landlords must digitally disclose extensive property details including square footage, amenities, and net rent for the city's 1.75 million apartments. (B.Z. – Die Stimme Berlins)

item.title: 🛢️ Oil cutoff, Lower crime, Jet fuel - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Russia will suspend the transit of Kazakh crude oil to Germany via the Druzhba pipeline starting May 1. Moscow has circulated updated export schedules reflecting the halt. The disruption affects the pipeline’s northern branch, cutting off deliveries that reached 2.15 million tonnes in 2025. German operators say they have already been notified of transport disruptions. (Reuters)

item.title: 🪲 Tick on the rise, Power cuts, Asylum applications - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Milder winters are causing tick season to start earlier in Berlin and Brandenburg. The parasites are now active almost year-round, driving a surge in diseases. By mid-April 2026, Brandenburg recorded 161 borreliosis infections, while Berlin found 83. This early spike follows a record-breaking 2025, when Berlin registered 1,406 borreliosis cases after a wet summer created ideal breeding conditions in local forests and parks. (rbb24)

item.title: 🏳️‍🌈 CSD expansion, Aggresive fox, Ancient coins - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin's Christopher Street Day (CSD) will expand into a two-day event for the first time. Organizers announced a "Democracy Evening" at the Brandenburg Gate for Friday, July 24, 2026. It features political speeches and music ahead of the traditional Saturday parade. The expansion responds directly to extremist actions and aims to defend human rights. (WELT)

item.title: 🏗️ RAW-Gelände Permits, Speed checks, Tourist litter - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Clubs at the RAW-Gelände in Friedrichshain are currently operating under temporary permits, putting their long-term future at risk. A local tenant association has launched a campaign calling for 30-year lease agreements to protect the area’s cultural venues from displacement. Negotiators are working to establish a general leaseholder model that would provide legal certainty for the affected spaces. (Berliner Zeitung)

item.title: 🚫 Mock executions, Purchasing power, AfD manifesto - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The assembly authority in Berlin has officially banned the public depiction of executions and killings at all demonstrations. Under the newly tightened regulations, protesters are strictly prohibited from staging realistic death scenes, restraining individuals, or carrying props designed to simulate lethal violence. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🎓 Student deportation, Smartphone data, Flower pots - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Hundreds of Indian students enrolled in hybrid programs at Berlin’s International University (IU) are facing sudden deportation. The Berlin State Office for Immigration has ruled that these predominantly online courses do not meet the legal requirements for a full-time, in-person student residence permit. As a result, authorities are rejecting and revoking visas, forcing affected students to either leave Germany or complete their degrees remotely. (Forschung & Lehre)

item.title: 🚲 Bike parking, Digital payments, Military travel rules - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin is heavily expanding bicycle parking at transit stations to satisfy commuter demand. The state-owned firm Infravelo plans to build 4,100 new spots over the next two years. A flagship project at Schöneweide station will feature a two-story wooden garage offering 1,100 spaces when it opens in 2027. Planners project the city needs 42,000 additional spots by 2030 to hit its infrastructure targets. (Berliner Kurier)

item.title: 🕺 Dancing bans, Cannabis evaluation, Tempelhof shelter - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Public dancing and loud entertainment are temporarily banned across Berlin and Brandenburg this Easter weekend. Under German law, Good Friday is designated a protected “silent holiday,” requiring venues to pause music and celebrations. In Berlin, the restriction applies from 4 a.m. to 9 p.m. on Friday. (rbb24)

item.title: 📊 Inflation rise, Healthcare deficit, Merz backlash - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Inflation in Berlin and Brandenburg picked up in March 2026, reaching 2.5% and 2.8% year-on-year respectively, largely driven by rising energy costs amid tensions linked to the Iran conflict. Energy prices alone jumped 6.7% in Berlin and 8.5% in Brandenburg. Without heating oil and fuel, inflation would be significantly lower at around 1.8–1.9%. (rbb24)

item.title: 🇩🇪 Youth in Germany, Neukölln shooting, Deepfakes - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The 2026 “Youth in Germany” study paints a stark picture of growing disillusionment among 14- to 29-year-olds, driven by global crises and economic pressure. Around 21% are actively planning to leave the country, while 29% say they need psychological support due to severe stress and exhaustion. (FOCUS online)

item.title: 🏃‍♂️ 40,000 Runners, Hamas sentences, Fuel prices - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The 45th Berlin Half Marathon returns this Sunday, bringing significant traffic disruptions to the city center. More than 40,000 participants from over 130 nations will navigate the 21.097-kilometer course. The main event starts near the Brandenburg Gate, while Saturday features a new 1.6-kilometer Garmin Berlin Mile race. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🚓 Police raids, Increased safety, Water shortage - Berlin News Daily
item.description: German police conducted large-scale raids on Tuesday targeting suspects linked to a September 2025 arson attack on Berlin’s power grid. Approximately 500 officers searched 18 locations across Berlin, Hamburg, and North Rhine-Westphalia, focusing on anarchist sites like the "Kalabal!k" library in Kreuzberg. (DW)

item.title: 🏗️ Skyscraper plans, Afghan embassy, Görli threats - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin has opened a public consultation (until April 14) for a 167-meter residential tower at Warschauer Brücke. The project on Rudolfstraße includes around 1,000 apartments, with 30% set aside as social housing. Local district officials have called the tower a “disaster” for the neighborhood and a blow to local democracy. (rbb24)

item.title: 🫶 Refugee support cuts, Cocaine use, Mental health decline - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin registered around 177,000 refugees at the end of 2025, which is roughly 5% of the city’s population. Integration Commissioner Katarina Niewiedzial reported that Ukrainians remain the largest demographic with over 57,000 residents. Officials noted positive integration trends, pointing to rising employment rates among Syrian nationals and an increasing number of refugee students. (rbb24)

item.title: ✈️ Airport strikes, Apprenticeship levy, AI capacity - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin-Brandenburg Airport will halt all passenger flights on Wednesday, March 18, due to a warning strike organized by the Verdi union. The walkout affects around 2,000 employees in key operational roles. As a result, all 445 scheduled departures and arrivals have been canceled, disrupting travel for roughly 57,000 passengers. (Travel And Tour World)

item.title: 🔑 Die Linke campaign, Shooting in Neukölln, Climate goals - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin's Left Party (Die Linke) launched its 2026 election campaign with a strict focus on lowering housing costs. Lead candidate Elif Eralp proposed an immediate rent cap for state-owned apartments and a new tax on luxury villas valued over four million euros. The party explicitly positions itself as the primary challenger to Mayor Kai Wegner's conservative-led government. (Berliner Morgenpost)

item.title: 🛴 E-scooter ban, Crime stats, Oil reserves - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin is ending free-floating e-scooter parking on sidewalks, transitioning the city to a system of mandatory parking stations. The local Senate reached this agreement with the regional association for the blind to eliminate widespread tripping hazards. Officials plan to implement the ban district by district, aiming for a complete transition across the capital by 2035. (Berliner Kurier)

item.title: 🍺 Outdoor dining, Referendum results, Labor shortage - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The Berlin Senate passed a draft law on Tuesday to extend outdoor dining hours in designated nightlife districts. Venues will be permitted to serve guests outside until 11 p.m. from Sunday to Thursday, and until midnight on weekends and before public holidays. The reform replaces a 1970 federal law to establish tailored regulations for the capital. (rbb24)

item.title: 📣 Women's Day March, Doner prices, Period products - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Tens of thousands took to the streets of Berlin on Sunday to mark International Women’s Day. The largest demonstration, organized by an alliance of trade unions and civic initiatives, drew around 30,000 participants. Marchers headed to the Rotes Rathaus, Berlin’s city hall, under the slogan “Feminist, in solidarity, trade unionist.” (Ntv)

item.title: 🚦 Traffic accidents, Google hub, Exam fails - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police recorded more than 137,000 traffic accidents in 2025, a 3% increase year over year. Despite the rise in collisions, traffic fatalities fell to 37, down from 55 in 2024. Authorities also reported a slight decline in severe injuries. (rbb24)

item.title: 💰 Asset seizures, Leaving Germany, Fuel prices - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Senate approved a legal initiative proposing a reversal of the burden of proof for confiscating suspicious assets, led by Justice Senator Felor Badenberg. Under the draft law, authorities could seize cash or luxury goods if a suspect’s wealth is "grossly disproportionate" to their legal income. Currently, prosecutors must prove illicit origins beyond a reasonable doubt to permanently confiscate property. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: ⚠️ Heightened Security, March changes, Asylum reform - Berlin News Daily
item.description: German security authorities have adjusted the risk assessment for Jewish and Israeli institutions following recent military developments in Iran. While officials describe the current risk as elevated, security agencies noted there is currently no evidence of specific or imminent attack plans. Intelligence reports continue to monitor the potential for retaliatory activities within Europe by regional actors. (Yahoo)

item.title: 🛩️ Attack drones, AfD surges, Phone scams - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Germany’s parliamentary budget committee has approved an initial €540 million order for thousands of attack drones, often called “kamikaze drones,” from domestic startups Helsing and Stark Defence. The defense ministry had originally proposed a long-term €4.3 billion framework, but lawmakers limited the contract to €2 billion to keep closer control over spending. (Reuters)

item.title: 🚊 48h BVG strike, Citizenship rejections, Trade deficits - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The Verdi trade union announced a nationwide 48-hour warning strike affecting local public transport in most German federal states, including Berlin's BVG. From 3 a.m. Friday, February 27, until Sunday morning, subways, trams, and buses will stop running, though Deutsche Bahn's S-Bahn and regional trains remain operational. (rbb24)

item.title: 🏠 Women’s shelter, Bus crash, Golden bear - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin opened a new protection center for women and children fleeing domestic violence (Frauenhaus) in the southern part of the city. The 2.5 million euro facility features ten apartments with 26 family spots and already houses 18 women and 13 children. Its location remains confidential to ensure the safety of residents. (rbb24)

item.title: 🌳 Görli curfew, Escalator shutdowns, Anti-democratic shifts - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Starting March 1, Berlin’s Görlitzer Park will close nightly to combat crime. If you go through Görli at night, expect detours around the fenced entrances. The Senate administration confirmed that private security firms will lock the newly installed gates between 10 p.m. and 6 a.m. during winter months, shifting to an 11 p.m. start time in summer. (rbb24)

item.title: 📉 Population shrink, Taxi licenses, Litter fines - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A new analysis by the ifo Institute predicts Germany’s population will shrink by 10% by 2070, a much steeper decline than the previously estimated 1%. Researchers attribute this dramatic correction to updated data from the 2022 Census, which revealed the current population is actually 81.9 million rather than higher earlier projections. (Spiegel)

item.title: 🔫 Gun violence, Public sector raises, Greens campaign - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A 37-year-old man was critically injured on Thursday evening after an unknown assailant fired multiple shots into his vehicle from the sidewalk in Berlin-Tegel. Hours later, police discovered bullet holes in the windows of an apartment building in Kreuzberg, though no injuries occurred in this second overnight incident. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🍰 Cocaine cakes, Tesla-union clash, BASF relocation - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Police officials investigated a drug ring suspected of trafficking over 30 kilograms of cocaine from Berlin to Prague inside cream-filled cakes. Investigators stated the group concealed one-kilogram bricks of narcotics within the pastries and diligently covered the incisions with whipped cream to completely hide the tampering. (rbb24)

item.title: ⚠️ Wednesday strikes, Gang violence, Organic food - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Public sector employees across Berlin and Brandenburg will stage warning strikes on Wednesday, affecting daycare centers, schools, and police departments. The trade union Verdi organized a major demonstration in Potsdam where workers will march from the Lustgarten to the negotiation venue. Berlin's public transport operator BVG is excluded from this specific walkout. (rbb24)

item.title: 🧊 Freezing rain, AfD youth, Iran demonstrations - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin Brandenburg Airport suspended all flight operations Thursday night and Friday morning due to persistent freezing rain and black ice on runways. While takeoffs resumed by midday Friday, officials reported that de-icing procedures were taking up to an hour. That is twice the usual time required. (DW.com)

item.title: ✈️ Flights halted, Amazon fine, Gulf tour - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Departures at Berlin Brandenburg Airport came to a complete standstill on Thursday morning after rapidly freezing rain rendered aircraft de-icing operations impossible. While incoming flights continued to land safely, ground crews could not treat outgoing planes until conditions improved late in the morning. Officials have warned passengers to anticipate massive delays and ongoing flight cancellations. (The Berliner)

item.title: 📣 More strikes, Poverty risk, Frozen body found - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Major unions including Verdi and the GEW have called for a comprehensive warning strike across Berlin and Brandenburg on February 11. This coordinated walkout will affect state-run daycares, schools, police, and administration offices to coincide with the start of the third negotiation round in Potsdam. (rbb24)

item.title: 🛑 BVG strike, Free gravel, Sidewalk salt - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Commuters would face significant disruptions on Monday as the trade union Verdi launches a 24-hour warning strike starting at 3:00 a.m. The action halts most BVG subways and buses, though Deutsche Bahn's S-Bahn and regional trains remain unaffected. While passengers are barred from boarding, the BVG will operate empty trams to prevent overhead lines from freezing. (rbb24)

item.title: 💰 Subletting profit, Disaster response, Storm paralysis - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin officials agreed to standardize disaster protection across all districts following a recent major power outage. The Senate will fund new technical equipment like satellite phones and increase dedicated staff from two to five per district. Authorities also introduced "KatHelfer", a digital system to coordinate volunteers. (rbb24)

item.title: 🥶 Trams restart, €1M reward, Berlin climate pact - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin's tram network began resuming operations Tuesday after freezing rain caused a complete shutdown of the system earlier in the week. BVG restored service on key Metro lines following extensive manual de-icing efforts. While some lines like the M1 and M5 have returned to partial service, officials warned that irregular schedules and cancellations remain likely through Wednesday. (Berliner Zeitung)

item.title: 🌫️ Air polution, Snow warnings, Teen electrocuted - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Air quality in Berlin and Brandenburg dropped to "very poor" levels this weekend as monitoring stations recorded sharp spikes in fine dust. In Berlin-Neukölln, PM2.5 levels (tiny particles in the air that are 2.5 micrometers or smaller in diameter) reached 57 micrograms per cubic meter (µg/m³), while Potsdam hit 61 µg/m³, far exceeding the World Health Organization's daily guideline of 15 µg/m³. Authorities attribute the hazardous haze to accumulating local and regional emissions. (rbb24)

item.title: 🚲 Nextbike cuts, Spy arrest, Tesla layoffs - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Higher Administrative Court ruled that Nextbike must immediately remove its 6,500 rental bikes from city streets. The decision follows a legal dispute after the company failed to obtain a required special use permit. Nextbike continued operating privately after its public contract expired last summer, a move the court declared unauthorized (The Berliner)

item.title: 🚆 S-bahn vandalism, Daycare tragedy, EV charging - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Thousands of commuters in Berlin must endure significant travel delays and cancellations after vandals damaged critical signaling infrastructure on the S-Bahn network. Services on lines S1, S2, S25, S26, and S85 remain severely restricted between Gesundbrunnen and Nordbahnhof until full operations are scheduled to resume Thursday at 3:30 a.m. A temporary shuttle train is currently running every 20 minutes between Bornholmer Straße and Nordbahnhof. (Berliner Kurier)

item.title: 🇬🇱 Greenland exit, Foreign criminals, Sick leave - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A 15-member German military team left Greenland on Sunday, ending a brief deployment that began Friday. The experts arrived in Nuuk at Denmark's invitation to scout for future NATO exercises. While Bild called the withdrawal a "secret" order from Berlin, the Ministry of Defense stated the mission was successfully completed. (Der Spiegel)

item.title: 🏘️ Housing shortage, Corruption fears, Crisis management - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A new analysis by the Pestel Institute reveals a shortage of approximately 56,000 apartments in Berlin as of late 2024. Commissioned by the "Social Housing" alliance, the report indicates this deficit contributes to a nationwide record of 1.4 million missing homes. The demand is projected to rise significantly in coming years, exacerbating pressure on the capital's housing market. (WELT)

item.title: 🛡️ Security boost, Black ice, Affordable food - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin Mayor Kai Wegner announced that private security firms will immediately replace police in guarding the city’s power grid following the major arson attack on January 3rd. The Vulkangruppe (Volcano Group), a left-wing radical organization, has claimed responsibility, citing opposition to "AI data centers" and fossil fuel dependency. (rbb24)

item.title: ⚡ Grid attack crackdown, Resident emigration, Car-free referendums - Berlin News Daily
item.description:
Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt vowed to "strike back" against extremists following an arson attack on Berlin’s power grid by the Volcano Groups. The sabotage left 100,000 residents freezing without electricity. Dobrindt announced a security package demanding increased intelligence staffing and expanded digital surveillance powers to track suspects more effectively.
(Yahoo)

item.title: 📢 Union strikes, Storm Elli, New grid threats - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Major trade unions have called for public sector warning strikes in Berlin and Brandenburg on January 14 and 15. Verdi, GEW, GdP, and IG Bau are mobilizing employees in schools, universities, police stations, and fire departments. While school staff will walk out on Wednesday, university and daycare centers face disruptions on both days. (rbb24)

item.title: 🏨 Blackout victims reimbursed, Overwhelmed hospitals, Maduro - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin officials announced they will retroactively reimburse hotel costs for Steglitz-Zehlendorf residents displaced by the ongoing power outage. Affected citizens must submit their hotel invoices and proof of residence to the district Social Welfare Office to receive the funds, replacing the previous system where guests had to pay a discounted €70 rate themselves. (rbb24)

item.title: 🔌 Power Cuts, Severe Frosts, Flight Cancellations - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A far-left activist organization known as the "Volcano Group" claimed responsibility for an arson attack on a high-voltage cable bridge in Berlin on Saturday. The sabotage cut electricity to approximately 50,000 households and 2,000 businesses, primarily in the city's southwest districts. The perpetrators stated their goal was to target the fossil fuel industry, specifically the Lichterfelde gas-fired power plant. (The New York Sun)

item.title: 🧨 New Year's aftermath, Policy changes, Climate protection - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police arrested nearly 430 individuals during New Year's Eve celebrations. Officials described it as relatively controlled despite isolated rioting. While 25,000 people gathered peacefully at the Brandenburg Gate, officers in districts like Neukölln and Kreuzberg faced targeted attacks with pyrotechnics that injured 35 service personnel. (DIE ZEIT)

item.title: 🎆 New Year's Eve, Bus arson, Bodycam rollout - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin will deploy 4,300 police officers this New Year’s Eve, a massive increase from the usual 1,000, to prevent riots and targeted attacks on emergency services. Authorities have expanded firework prohibition zones to include the Admiralbrücke, alongside existing bans in Steinmetzkiez and Sonnenallee. Police will also use preventive custody for up to five days against aggressors. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: ❄️ Black ice, Lyme disease, Affordable housing - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin and Brandenburg faced dangerous road conditions this weekend as freezing rain turned into "black ice." Police in the capital reported 565 traffic accidents between Friday afternoon and Saturday morning, a significant jump from the usual 152 reports. (rbb24)

item.title: 🎆 Firework bans, Construction shortfall, Asphalt removal - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police have designated specific zones where fireworks and weapons are banned for the 2025/26 New Year's Eve celebrations. From 6 p.m. on December 31 to 6 a.m. on January 1, a new zone at Admiralsbrücke in Kreuzberg joins existing restricted areas like Alexanderplatz and Steinmetzkiez. The order prohibits Category F2, F3, and F4 pyrotechnics, as well as knives, though smaller Category F1 items remain legal. Mayor Kai Wegner pledged strict enforcement against offenders but emphasized that a complete sales ban requires federal legislation. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 💰 Aid repayments, Renate saved, Miles fined €25M - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s state bank IBB expects to reclaim €325.5 million in pandemic aid from thousands of freelancers and small business owners. While the 2020 grants provided a critical lifeline, roughly 15,000 cases have already been flagged for partial or full repayment. For many recipients, these massive financial demands now pose a direct threat of private insolvency and long-term economic instability. (Berliner Morgenpost)

item.title: 🚓 Police at New Year's, Deutschlandticket, Medical cannabis - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin authorities are preparing for a challenging New Year's Eve, deploying around 4,000 police officers and 1,500 firefighters. Four designated zones, including Alexanderplatz and Sonnenallee, will enforce a ban on fireworks, knives, and weapons. Police have already seized 140,000 illegal pyrotechnics, over two-thirds of which are dangerous F4 category items like sphere bombs. (rbb24.de)

item.title: 🏠 Housing racism, Deportations continue, Subletting scrutiny - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A new study reveals significant racial discrimination in Germany’s housing market. Research from the German Center for Integration and Migration Research shows Black and Muslim people are far more likely to be excluded from viewings. In a test, résumés with German-sounding names received viewing invitations 22% of the time, versus 16% for other names. (InfoMigrants)

item.title: 🕊️ Ukraine Peace Talks, Public service strike, ADHD surge - Berlin News Daily
item.description: US officials are traveling to Berlin for peace talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky and European leaders. The discussions will center on a US-proposed peace plan, which reportedly requires Ukraine to cede territory and renounce NATO ambitions. Territorial concessions in the Donbas region remain a primary obstacle, alongside proposals for a new demilitarized zone. (New York Post)

item.title: 🍣 Tuna recall, Emergency pillars, Illegal rent - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The manufacturer Sicily Food is recalling its "Sansibar Deluxe Thunfisch Carpaccio" sold at Lidl stores in 11 German states. The recall is due to contamination with Listeria monocytogenes bacteria. It affects 100g packages with use-by dates of December 27, 28, and 29, 2025. Customers can return the product for a full refund without a receipt. (rbb24)

item.title: 📈 Minimum wage rise, Rental socialization, Student protests - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Senate has approved a statutory instrument raising the state minimum wage from €13.69 to €14.84 per hour on January 1, 2026, and to €15.59 in 2027. The rule covers sectors where Berlin has financial influence, including the civil service, state-owned firms, and publicly funded social welfare organizations. (Berlin.de)

item.title: 💰 Tax fraud, High-rises, Electric cars - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Senate Finance Administration carried out cash register inspections at only 1.2 % of the city’s roughly 80,300 cash‑only businesses in 2024. That means almost 99 of 100 such shops, restaurants, or service providers were not checked. The administration also lacks figures on violations, fines, and prior‑year comparisons. (Berliner Morgenpost)

item.title: 🖲️ New surveillance law, S15 launch, Berlin’s governance crisis - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s state parliament has passed a major overhaul of the city’s police law, the Allgemeines Sicherheits- und Ordnungsgesetz. CDU, SPD, and AfD approved measures that expand police powers to monitor phones and laptops, secretly enter and search homes, conduct online searches of devices, use state spyware, activate bodycams in apartments, deploy drones, and run facial recognition. (Berliner Zeitung)

item.title: 🌳 Green party plan, Kitas closing, Aid for the homeless - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Green party has presented a 125‑page draft platform for the September 2026 Abgeordnetenhaus election under the slogan “Change politics, keep Berlin.” The program prioritizes housing, including stricter limits on holiday rentals, more support for housing cooperatives, and a “Affordable Rents Law” requiring large landlords to offer a share of flats at lower prices. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🥡 Subcontractor ban, Rent control limits, Fireworks - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Germany’s labour minister Bärbel Bas plans to examine a ban on subcontractors for food delivery platforms. The move follows rbb findings of forced undeclared work, unpaid wages, threats, and violence among riders working for firms supplying Wolt and Uber Eats. Bas argues that only direct employment can ensure transparency and compliance with labour law. (tagesschau.de)

item.title: 🚨 Seized weapons, HIV prevention drug, Fake police - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police have launched a special commission, "Soko Ferrum," to combat a surge in gang shootings and protection rackets. Since mid-November, officials have seized 32 illegal weapons and made 193 arrests following checks on over 3,300 individuals. Police Chief Barbara Slowik Meisel identified extortion against businesses as the primary driver of the violence. Despite facing violent resistance (including a recent raid leaving four officers injured) authorities have declared the crackdown a top priority to dismantle criminal networks and remove illegal firearms. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🚆 Transit app errors, QR drug deals, Nursing Staff Relief - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A report from rbb24 describes how Berlin and Brandenburg passengers regularly receive incorrect real time information from public transport apps. The apps, including BVG, S-Bahn and Google Maps, all use data from the Verkehrsverbund Berlin-Brandenburg, which aggregates timetable and punctuality data from 37 companies. Manual data entry during disruptions often causes errors and ghost vehicles. (rbb24)

item.title: 💸 Excessive spending, Child poverty, Far-right ideas - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Court of Audit warns that the city has spent about €2 billion more each year than it earns for years, and says the draft 2026/27 budget would fully deplete reserves within two years. The report also criticizes weak disaster preparedness, delays at the Molkenmarkt housing project, and lost revenue from waived sidewalk fees. (rbb24)

item.title: 🌿 Damaged forests, December changes, Naturalization complaints - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s 2025 Forest Condition Report finds 46 % of the city’s forest area shows significant damage, the highest level since monitoring began. Only 3 % of trees are undamaged. Pine is hit hardest, with 40 % badly damaged and just 3 % healthy, while two‑thirds of oaks remain severely affected despite slight improvement. (berlin.de)

item.title: 🌡️ Heat action plan, Temporary rentals, Rising sick leave - Berlin News Daily
item.description: The Berlin Senate has adopted a new “heat action plan” to better protect residents from rising temperatures. The plan combines 72 measures, some already underway, including “Cooling Points” in public buildings for rest and hydration, more drinking fountains, additional street trees and green spaces, and heat helpers in large housing estates. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 📬 Document collection, Heating faliure, Snow in Berlin - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin has introduced its first 24/7 document collection box at the Eichborndamm Bürgeramt (citizens’ office) in Reinickendorf. Residents must still apply in person for passports, ID cards, and residence permits, but they now receive a PIN by mobile phone and can pick up finished documents from the box at any time without an appointment. (IamExpat)

item.title: 🏠 Rents double, Firecracker ban, Driving school extortion - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s advertised rents have almost doubled in a decade. According to figures in the official gazette, average asking rents in Berlin rose from €8.45 per m² in 2015 to €16.64 in 2024, a jump of 96.9 %. They now sit 52.4 % above the national average, while Germany overall saw a 49.6 % rise in asking rents. (rbb24)

item.title: 🚨 School terror threat, Görli security, EV charging - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin experienced a wave of terror panic on Sunday and Monday after a YouTuber spread an unverified claim about planned attacks on 20 schools. Instead of alerting police and waiting for clarification, he shared the rumor publicly, triggering citywide anxiety and highlighting how emotional reactions and online reach can amplify false information. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🏛️ Civil servant pay, Discontinued trains, Pensions disputes - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Germany’s Federal Constitutional Court has ruled that the pay of many Berlin civil servants was largely unconstitutional between 2008 and 2020. The decision fits a broader pattern of pay disputes in the public sector. The court and higher administrative courts have repeatedly criticized Berlin’s remuneration since at least 2015. Germany’s top court now says about 95 % of Berlin’s salary groups from 2008 to 2020 breached constitutional standards, and Berlin must fix the system by 31 March 2027. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🚯 Illegal waste, Facial recognition, BVG reports - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin has sharply increased penalties for littering and illegal dumping after the Senate approved a new fine catalog on November 4, now published in the Amtsblatt. Cigarette butt fines rise to €250–€3,000, bulky items like mattresses can cost up to €11,000, and hazardous construction or chemical waste can reach €100,000 per case. (Berlin.de)

item.title: 🚇 Snipers in Berlin, Friedrichstrasse revamp, Nuclear reactors - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin will host parts of a Bundeswehr exercise next week. The Wachbataillon will conduct “Bollwerk Bärlin III” from November 17 to 21, training urban combat and protection of defense‑critical infrastructure. Drills include U‑Bahn Jungfernheide from 1 a.m. to 4 a.m. on November 18–20, with staged snipers, and activities in Ruhleben and Rüdersdorf. Military convoys may cause detours. (Berliner Zeitung)

item.title: 🚗 Car-first budget, Power grid repairs, Increased drug consumption - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin is renewing a car-first approach as the current CDU and SDP coalition scales back cycling and pedestrian plans. Speed limits on more than 20 streets returned to 50 kilometers per hour. Budgets for school-route safety and sidewalks face deep cuts. The A100 autobahn extension and Torstrasse redesign fuel protests and division. (The Guardian)

item.title: 🏛️ Special fund plan, Google investment, Airport personalization - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s CDU and SPD agreed on a plan to use federal special-fund money. The city expects 5.2 billion euros over twelve years, flowing from January 1, 2025 to December 31, 2036. Earmarks include 700 million for the climate adaptation law, 300 million for water infrastructure, 680 million for trains, 170 million for bridges, and other civic upgrades. (rbb24)

item.title: 🏥 Hospital explosions, Tempelhof renovation, Wildlife network - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Overnight into Tuesday, two major Berlin hospitals reported a suspected arson and a detonation affecting cancer radiation facilities. At Vivantes Klinikum Neukölln in Buckow, a strong explosion shattered several windows and damaged the entrance to the radiotherapy department; police suspect illegal pyrotechnics. State security investigators and explosives experts are involved, and the Kormoranweg/Rotschwanzweg area was cordoned off.

item.title: 🚫 Prostitution ban, Winter jackets, Fake police - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Bundestag President Julia Klöckner is pushing to ban prostitution and sex purchases in Germany, backed by Health Minister Nina Warken, who favors a "Nordic model" targeting clients. This reopens debate on Germany's 2002 legalization, which critics argue failed to stop exploitation. Many sex workers, estimated in the thousands, remain unregistered. (DW)

item.title: 🛍️ Sunday sales ban, Housing and relationships, Deutschlandticket funding - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s retail association renewed calls to liberalize Sunday shopping. In an interview, Handelsverband Berlin-Brandenburg chief Nils Busch-Petersen argued that Germany’s general Sunday sales ban is no longer timely. He criticized Berlin’s administration for scheduling only four of eight permitted citywide open Sundays. He said such limits disadvantage physical retailers compared with other sales channels. Many employees volunteer for Sunday shifts because pay is higher and average receipts increase. (Berliner Zeitung)

item.title: 👮 Gun violence, Car-free referendum, QR drug sales - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police are targeting a surge in gun violence, with over 400 shooting incidents recorded by late October, already surpassing the total for 2024. Authorities believe the spike is caused by turf wars among criminal groups, often over drug territories and dominance, fueled by significant profits. (rbb24)

item.title: 🌳 A million trees, Spandau cannabis bust, Asylum seeker return - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s House of Representatives passed a Climate Adaptation Act committing the city to one million trees by 2040, up from about 440,000 today. Backed by all parties except Alternative for Germany and based on the BaumEntscheid initiative, it makes Berlin the first German state with such a law. The €3.2 billion plan expands green spaces, unseals pavements, and manages rainwater. (The Local Germany)

item.title: 🧪 Contaminated water, Excessive rents, Increased waste fines - Berlin News Daily
item.description: An environmental group found per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) in almost all tested water samples in Berlin and Brandenburg. Nationwide, 42 of 46 samples contained PFAS. Current legal limits were not exceeded. Some readings (in Zeuthen) surpassed new limits that take effect from January 2026. Berlin’s utility said tap water remains safe and treatment is in place at Tegel. (rbb24)

item.title: 🚨 Bomb equipment detained, BER noise protection, Military draft checks - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin prosecutors said a 22-year-old Syrian man was arrested on Saturday on suspicion of preparing a jihadi-motivated attack. Special Task Force officers detained him after searches at three addresses found equipment to build a bomb. Officials said he arrived in 2023 and shared Islamic State propaganda online. Ministers Alexander Dobrindt and Iris Spranger urged vigilance. (DW)

item.title: 🎓 Entry-level struggles, Sadistic Dentists, Energy sharing - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Many German graduates are finding first jobs harder to land than vocational peers, rbb24 reports. Alisa Sogijaine sent 40–50 applications before securing a trainee role at energy provider Edis, where applications per opening have doubled. Entry-level postings for academics fell 43.9% in Berlin and 54.7% in Brandenburg; young graduate unemployment also rose. (rbb24)

item.title: ☕ LAP cafés, Drug lab discovered, A100 closures - Berlin News Daily
item.description: LAP (“Life Among People”) is a Berlin startup offering cheap, automated coffee from minimalist cafés across trendy neighborhoods. Founded in 2023 by Delivery Hero and Flink alumni, it aims to make good coffee affordable and social, using influencer marketing and hosting community events. With prices far below traditional cafés and funding from major investors, LAP has rapidly expanded to 16 Berlin locations and beyond. However, critics accuse it of fueling gentrification, undercutting small cafés, and relying on disposable cups. In late October, all LAP cafés were vandalized, reflecting growing backlash against its perceived commercialization of Berlin’s coffee culture. (Berliner Morgenpost)

item.title: 🚨 Burglar tricks, Military site at Tegel, Phantom diagnoses - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police warned residents yesterday about near-invisible threads stuck between door and frame, a method burglars use to test if homes are empty during the autumn school holidays. Reports are clustering in Prenzlauer Berg, with flyers urging documentation and removal. Break-ins rose: 1,395 cases in summer 2025, up from 982 in 2024 and 841 in 2023. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🏚️ Homeless tours, Women’s protection cuts, Expropriation push - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Yesterday, The Berliner discussed guided walks led by formerly unhoused Berliners as a response to calls to deport homeless European Union nationals. On a route by Ostbahnhof and Berghain, guide Miriam recounts surviving domestic abuse, bottle-collecting, and safer spots to sleep, while project manager Clemens Poldrack warns of a hardening, antisocial political discourse. (The Berliner)

item.title: 🧬 Bird flu, Tree vandalism, Spring drought - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Brandenburg authorities said yesterday that roughly 130,000 birds must be culled at two poultry operations in Märkisch-Oderland after confirmed H5N1 outbreaks. The Friedrich-Loeffler-Institut also confirmed Berlin’s first cases this wave in two dead cranes, while mass die-offs near Linum have already reached about 1,200 birds, officials said. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🤔 Doorstep scams, Unusable public toilets, Clubs at risk - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berliners are grappling with pushy door-to-door visits, rbb24 reported yesterday. A TikTok user described two men claiming to be from Deutsche Telekom who tried to enter and even wedged a foot in her door. Police do not track fake company callers. Firms tout company badges, uniforms and quick response (QR) codes; telecoms never do unannounced router checks. (rbb24)

item.title: 🏠 Neukölln explosion, English-only shift, AI hub launch - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin authorities reported a deadly explosion in a Buckow single-family house yesterday. Around 19:00 on Monday, the fireplace suddenly exploded; police are probing why. A 41-year-old mother died at the scene; another adult and a child were seriously hurt, while three adults and three children suffered minor injuries. Windows shattered and the home was badly damaged, without a fire. (rbb24)

item.title: 🚲 Cyclist accidents, Friedrichstrasse decline, Asbestos at homes - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin discussed how to stop “dooring” crashes yesterday. ADFC Berlin chair Eberhard Brodhage said the city sees about two such collisions daily and cited the recent death of actress Wanda Perdelwitz. He urged 2.5‑meter bike lanes under the 2018 Mobility Act, traffic calming, the Dutch Reach, 1.5‑meter passing, and door‑warning tech. (rbb24)

item.title: 🚌 BVG Robotaxis, Kotti cleanup, Nextbike shutdown risk - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s public transport operator BVG launched pilot tests of autonomous shuttles in the city’s northwest yesterday. The NoWeL4 project puts five Volkswagen ID. Buzz AD vehicles with Level 4 technology on public roads, initially without passengers, across a 15-square-kilometre zone serving about 80 stops. The €9.5 million trial runs to 2027, with passenger pilots in early 2026. (Mobility Portal)

item.title: 🚇 U2 closure, Wildlife in Berlin, New night trains - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Yesterday, Berliner Morgenpost reported that from Monday, 20 October, passengers face restrictions on U-Bahn line U2. The section between Stadtmitte and Senefelderplatz is closed during the autumn holidays. Replacement buses detour via Jerusalemer Straße, Fischerinsel, and Rotes Rathaus, skipping Hausvogteiplatz, Märkisches Museum, and Klosterstraße. Work upgrades Alexanderplatz’s signal control and should finish by early November. (Berliner Morgenpost)

item.title: 📉 Berlin economic crisis, Cheaper driving licenses, Friedrichshain raid - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin companies see little light ahead: In a fall survey published yesterday, the Berlin Chamber of Industry and Commerce (IHK) and the Chamber of Crafts (HWK) reported worse business sentiment than in spring. Thirty percent of firms said exports fell—mainly due to United States tariffs—while services held steady; the groups warn of rising job cuts as unemployment already exceeds 10 percent. (Tagesspiegel)

item.title: 🎓 Rising student costs, Kreuzberg shooting, Kita closures - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Rising rents, semester fees and living costs are forcing many students in Berlin and Brandenburg into long commutes and 20-hour workweeks alongside full-time study. Student unions report a sharp rise in mental‑health consultations since 2020, and the German National Association for Student Affairs warns of “a new form of social selection” without faster reform and more dorm capacity. (rbb24)

item.title: 🐀 Rat infestations, BER security checks, Kreuzberg barriers - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin reported widespread rat infestations across nearly all districts, with thousands of annual complaints, according to a Senate response to a Green party inquiry.

item.title: 🏠 Excessive rents, Neo‑Nazis, Pension tax changes - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin issued its first finalized administrative fine for an excessive rent under Section 5 of the Economic Offenses Act, enabling a tenant in Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg to reclaim more than €22,000.

item.title: 🚨 Underage prostitution, Compulsory military service, Migration plan critique - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A Berlin state police commissioner said the unit dedicated to human trafficking and forced prostitution of minors is fully tasked, with most victims unwilling to be “rescued,” and exploitation occurring largely online or in hidden settings, making detection and prosecutions difficult.

item.title: 🏨 Hotel data leak, N8N raise $180M, Power-grid attacks - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A German media investigation found serious security flaws in hotel management software from Gubse AG that exposed millions of guest records online, including Motel One customers and some lawmakers; the vendor has since patched the vulnerabilities.

item.title: 🩺 Rising COVID cases, End of fast citizenship, Taliban flag - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Germany’s public health institute reported a seasonal uptick in acute respiratory illnesses and a low but rising level of COVID-19, with the Omicron-lineage variant “Stratus” accounting for about 80% of recent cases.

item.title: 📰 Fourth Hamas suspect in Berlin, Night of vaccinations, Nazi tunnels - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Germany's federal prosecutor confirmed a fourth suspect linked to a Berlin investigation into suspected Hamas members, and police searched his Treptow apartment overnight; no arrest was made.

item.title: 💼 Digital startup services, Low blood reserves, Government AI push - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin became the first German state to let founders complete both business and tax registrations in a single digital process via KfW’s Gründerplattform app, including direct data transfer to authorities and in‑app fee payment.

item.title: 🏥 Hospital wartime plan, Ryanair cuts, Senior scams - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s health authority and major hospitals drafted a confidential 26-page civil defense plan outlining how city hospitals would shift to wartime operations, including creating capacity for about 100 wounded soldiers daily, potential patient discharges, and triage prioritizing military personnel, while key funding and staffing questions remain unresolved.

item.title: 💸 “Zu verschenken” fines, Unpaid water bills, Airport delays - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin will introduce higher fines for leaving items on sidewalks, targeting illegal dumping and potentially curbing the city’s “zu verschenken” giveaway custom, with penalties from €150–€300 for household items in Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg and up to €15,000 for appliances.

item.title: 🚓 Hamas arrested in Berlin, Rent overcharge, Vietnamese trainees missing - Berlin News Daily
item.description: German police arrested three suspected Hamas members in Berlin for allegedly procuring weapons for assassinations targeting Israeli or Jewish institutions, prosecutors said.

item.title: 👀 AI surveillance, BER Cyber attack, Crash at Alexanderplatz - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Senate confirmed plans to pilot a surveillance AI that analyzes pedestrian behavior, following deployments in Mannheim and Hamburg.

item.title: 🌍 Lithium deposits, Cannabis consumption, House fire manhunt - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Neptune Energy says it has identified a major lithium deposit in the Altmark region of northern Saxony-Anhalt, estimating 43 million tonnes of lithium carbonate equivalent following a summer pilot project.

item.title: 🏛️ Mass Berlin protest, IBAN name checks, Station knife checks - Berlin News Daily
item.description: More than 100,000 people marched in Berlin to protest Germany’s support for Israel and the war in Gaza, calling for an end to arms exports and accusing Israel of genocide.

item.title: 🏠 Berlin socialises 220k, Huge Gaza protest, Sabotage at Deutsche Bahn - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s “Deutsche Wohnen & Co. Enteignen” initiative unveiled a draft law to transfer up to 220,000 apartments from large private landlords into public ownership, with compensation of €8B–€18B set below market value and financed over 100 years.

item.title: 📈 Planet Labs factory, Syphilis cases rise, Wildberger urges AI loosening - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Planet Labs said it will open its first satellite factory outside California in Berlin, investing €45M to produce its high-resolution Pelican satellites and adding about 70 jobs.

item.title: 🏛️ Broadcasting fee exemptions, Mass naturalization questions, One-third drink harmfully - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Germany will expand exemptions to its public broadcasting fee on Oct. 1, 2025, allowing more low-income residents—including BAföG-funded students living away from their parents—to be excused from the €18.36 monthly charge.

item.title: 🏠 Berlin sublet rent-cap test, Heating costs jump, AfD overtakes Union - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Germany’s Federal Court of Justice is hearing a Berlin case on whether the rent cap applies to sublets, after a tenant sublet a €460 apartment for €962 without permission; the landlord seeks termination.

item.title: ✈️ BER cyber delays, Berlin police overhaul, job losses soar - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Three days after a cyberattack on IT provider Collins Aerospace, Berlin Brandenburg Airport reported widespread disruption Monday, with about 70% of departures delayed—many by over an hour—long queues, and at least one cancellation, as staff handled check-in and baggage using manual processes.

item.title: 🏛️ 48-hour workweek plan, Cannabis Act backlash, Styles sub-3h marathon - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Germany’s government plans to overhaul the Working Hours Act by replacing daily limits with a 48-hour weekly cap, prompting sharp criticism from the Hugo Sinzheimer Institute.

item.title: 🏠 Student rents +74%, Club assaults rise, Airports disrupted by cyberattack - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A study by the research institute Empirica finds that rents for rooms in student flat-shares in Germany have risen 74% since 2012, roughly twice the increase in consumer prices, with major city gaps (about €750 in Munich versus €350 in Siegen).

item.title: 🌊 Berlin water shortages and recycling, rising crime hotspots, Spandau WWII bomb defused - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin is ramping up investments in modernized waterworks, wastewater recycling, and climate‑resilient trees to confront worsening water shortages as falling river and lake levels threaten shipping, industry, and drinking water supplies.

item.title: 🚨 WWII bomb off Fischerinsel, ASML opens diamond-coated wafer plant, Raid over illegal asbestos dumping - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police discovered a suspected World War II bomb in the Spree near Fischerinsel Thursday, establishing a 500-meter exclusion zone and launching overnight evacuations; U2 service is halted between Alexanderplatz and Spittelmarkt, while shelters opened at Rathaus Mitte as thousands of residents and hotel guests were told to leave.

item.title: 🚨 Spandau bomb defusal Sept 19, 6% Berlin-Brandenburg fare hike January, BVG autonomous buses 2035 - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Workers in Berlin’s Spandau district uncovered a 100-kilogram World War II aerial bomb on Neuendorfer Straße, with disposal scheduled for Friday, Sept. 19.

item.title: 🎵 Drake at Berghain, BER night‑flight curfew dispute, Berlin Marathon road closures - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Drake reportedly slipped into Berlin’s Berghain on his day off from three shows at the Uber Arena, friend and DJ-producer Gordo wrote on Instagram: “we got in,” saying Drake danced in the crowd without security; Gordo praised the club and noted Berghain’s strict no-photo rule. (Berliner Zeitung, 1 minute read)

item.title: ⚖️ Mutombo death: police faulted, Clan brothers convicted but one released, Tegel renovation delayed over funding - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s state anti‑discrimination ombudsperson (LADG) has formally faulted police for the 2022 death of Kupa Illunga Medard Mutombo, who lost consciousness during a Sept. 14 Spandau intervention and died weeks later. The report says officers violated anti‑discrimination rules over his disability and language, used prone restraint that increased asphyxia risk, and lacked medical help; prosecutors are investigating five officers and the ombudsperson demands an apology, at least €45,000 compensation and systemic reform. (rbb24, 2 minute read)

item.title: 🏥 Three Berlin knife attacks, 12,000 join pro-Palestinian rally, Berlin's first Currywurst Hawaii - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police reported three separate overnight knife attacks in Prenzlauer Berg, Staaken, and Kreuzberg, leaving a 53-year-old and a 59-year-old hospitalized and a 44-year-old critically injured; officers arrested a suspect in Staaken and questioned another in Prenzlauer Berg, while the Kreuzberg attacker remains unknown.

item.title: 🏛️ Huge Gaza protest in Berlin, Humboldt Forum fatal stabbing, Berlin drone grocery pilot - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police deployed about 1,000 officers as a large Gaza protest called by Sahra Wagenknecht began at the Brandenburg Gate, with up to 15,000 participants expected.

item.title: 🚨 Parked car explodes in Schöneberg, Burnt woman found in playground, ICC opens 49 hours - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A parked car exploded in Berlin’s Schöneberg overnight, seriously injuring a 22-year-old man; police and prosecutors say he survived because he wasn’t inside or next to it.

item.title: 🏠 Power outage after arson, Gaza rally at Brandenburger-Tor, Three Berlin women arrested for drug smuggling - Berlin News Daily
item.description: In Berlin’s Treptow-Köpenick, 13,700 customers remained without electricity for a third day, the grid operator said, with full restoration expected today but no precise timing.

item.title: 🚇 Berlin grid arson outage hits 13,700, Munich eases apartment swaps, Ant-hill stalls 2,500 homes - Berlin News Daily
item.description: An arson attack on two power pylons in Berlin’s Treptow-Köpenick caused a major blackout, with about 13,700 customers still without electricity Wednesday evening; grid operator Stromnetz Berlin expects full restoration during Thursday.

item.title: ⚖️ Daltons' seven-shot leg attack trial, VeloFlow predicts greens for cyclists, Berlin-Brandenburg lag in education rankings - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A Berlin court is trying three alleged members of the self-styled “Daltons of Berlin” for the March 30 shooting that left businessman Mustafa M., 36, with shattered leg bones; the accused shooter confessed to firing seven shots, while the others remain silent.

item.title: 🚨 20-year-old fires at supermarkets over extortion, 10.6M adults illiterate, Raccoon cull record in Berlin - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A 20-year-old man, identified as Alican C., went on trial in Berlin and confessed to shooting at two Eurogida supermarkets on March 3 following an extortion demand by a group calling itself the Butterflies.

item.title: ⚖️ PKK leadership trial after ticket inspector blinding, Charité reports rising street violence, Serial-killer exhibition - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Kammergericht opened the first trial in the city against an alleged PKK leadership figure since the group’s designation as a terrorist organization, charging Mehmet K., 50, with membership in a foreign terrorist organization and leadership roles in Germany since 2014.

item.title: 🚇 S-Bahn weekend closures for software upgrade, 240m Tempelhof skyscrapers proposed, Aurora season returns - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s S-Bahn suspended service on northern sections of lines S1, S2, and S8 for the weekend, with replacement buses running from Saturday 5:00 am to Monday 1:30 am.

item.title: 🚇 Shots fired at Berlin U8, Berlin tests sirens Sept 11, Major regional rail closures Sept–Dec - Berlin News Daily
item.description: An unidentified man fired a shot at a departing U8 subway train at Berlin’s Karl-Bonhoeffer-Nervenklinik station in Reinickendorf around 9 p.m. Thursday, police said; the projectile damaged a window but did not pierce it, and the shooter fled.

item.title: 🚗 BMW hits children, Budget cuts threaten HIV prevention, Districts warn parks collapse - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A BMW driver in Berlin’s Wedding district struck a group of 7- to 8-year-old children at a crossing Thursday, with six receiving medical attention—including three with minor injuries—and their caregiver seriously hurt; the driver was uninjured.

item.title: 🚇 Neukölln ear bite at U-Bahn, EHEC outbreak infects children, Sailing drowning prompts murder trial - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police said a 31-year-old man had part of his right ear bitten off during a dispute over headphones at the Leinestraße U-Bahn station in Neukölln on Tuesday night; the attacker fled empty-handed.

item.title: 🌡️ 75% jump in Lyme cases, Blind association sues over sidewalk e‑scooters, State offers tenant downsizing - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin recorded 41 new Lyme disease cases in the week of Aug. 18–24, bringing this year’s total to 862, 75% above the pre-pandemic median for the same period, the city’s health authority said.

item.title: 🏠 Rents up 30% via index leases, Homelessness spikes, ATM heist accomplice drowns - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin tenants face rent hikes up to 30% as index leases tie rents to the consumer price index, bypassing the city’s usual 15% three‑year cap, the Berliner Mieterverein warns. Index clauses let landlords raise rents with inflation—pushing the rent index higher and worsening the housing shortage—and the 2019 rent brake only applies at contract start. Tenants should verify calculations and documentation before paying; the Mieterverein urges introducing a cap on index increases. (Berliner Zeitung, 2 minute read)

item.title: 🏛️ Officer hits woman at protest, Berlin hospitals refitted as NATO hub, Refugees' trauma largely untreated - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin police are reviewing footage showing an officer repeatedly striking a woman in the face during a pro-Palestinian protest in central Berlin, after clashes between officers and demonstrators.

item.title: 🚗 A100 extension snarls traffic, few new teachers fully trained, arson at Green Party office - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s newly opened A100 extension toward Treptow has quickly clogged, with weekend backups and drivers taking about 20 minutes to travel the roughly 3-kilometer stretch; on Friday, officials briefly closed a tunnel to avoid stopped traffic.

item.title: ⚖️ Welfare recipient buys €300k drugs retrial, 16% new Berlin teachers qualified, Unemployment up Berlin/Brandenburg - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A Berlin court held a retrial of a man previously sentenced to three years and six months for large-scale drug purchases revealed via EncroChat, with prosecutors now seeking four years and the defense three.

item.title: ⚖️ Knife-attack trial, S-Bahn headed for crisis, Five-storey building evacuated over collapse risk - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A 19-year-old Afghan man went on trial at the Berlin regional court on charges including dangerous bodily harm, accused of taking part in a series of knife attacks across the city between October 2024 and February 2025; he remained silent at the opening hearing.

item.title: 🏠 98% Berlin rentals illegally high, nearly half drug samples conspicuous, new military service law - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s Rent Price Review Office said 93 of 95 rental contracts it assessed in Q2 2025 charged illegally high rents, including 61 at least 50% above the cap and one at 150%.

item.title: 🎤 Teen convicted for Taylor Swift concert plot, Neukölln bottle-and-bat attack, Dealer sold 22kg cocaine - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A 16-year-old was found guilty Tuesday in Berlin of planning a 2024 attack on a Taylor Swift concert and was sentenced to one year and six months' youth prison, suspended on probation. Authorities say the then-14-year-old, radicalized online by ISIS, helped an Austrian plotter arrested in Vienna in August 2024—translating bomb instructions, providing an ISIS loyalty text and contacting an ISIS member. The plot was foiled; the verdict can be appealed. (Tagesspiegel, 2 minute read)

item.title: 🚆 S-Bahn bus replacements in NE Berlin, Mohrenstraße renamed Anton‑Wilhelm‑Amo‑Straße, drones & greywater pilots - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Major S-Bahn disruptions hit northeast Berlin from early Monday as a new electronic signal box at Nordkreuz is commissioned: S8 trains between Pankow and Karow/Hohen Neuendorf will be replaced by accessible buses until the morning of 5 September; S2 and S26 are also suspended between Pankow and Karow with bus replacements. Separately, Blankenfelde–Mahlow has bus service until 22 September, and Ringbahn works mean buses run between Greifswalder Straße and Gesundbrunnen until 8 September (22:00); S-Bahn advises tram and U‑bahn alternatives. (rbb24, 2 minute read)

item.title: 🏛️ Six injured at Humboldt Forum stabbing, FDP politician hurt at protest, Dresden tram knife victim - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Six people were injured, one critically, in a knife attack near the Humboldt Forum in Berlin-Mitte on Saturday evening. Police say two groups of about eight–ten people clashed at the Karl-Liebknecht Bridge; victims (aged around 20–26) were hospitalized with stab and cut wounds. Investigations are ongoing into motives and whether the groups met earlier at Alexanderplatz; some participants may have fled. (rbb24, 2 minute read)

item.title: 🚗 A100 opens but Elsenbrücke closed, Man strangled in Reinickendorf pub, Catholic Church decries vandalism - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s controversial 3.2 km A100 extension between Neukölln and Treptower Park opens this Wednesday after 12 years and €720m of construction. Authorities say it will ease local streets and improve links to the A113, BER and Adlershof, but the unfinished Elsenbrücke (not due before 2028) and planned traffic metering risk shifting or increasing congestion; a further 17th section is already being planned. (rbb24, 3 minute read)

item.title: 🏥 Charité cuts endanger pediatric care, couriers paid cash without contracts, rain may cancel Keinemusik finale - Berlin News Daily
item.description: A one‑and‑a‑half‑year‑old at Berlin’s Charité waits for a donor heart as the hospital confronts a 25% cut to its investment budget and reductions in study places and midwifery training. Leaders warn the cuts—and a watered‑down federal hospital reform—threaten specialized pediatric care and research; officials say they will seek special funds while a new gene-and-cell therapy center nears completion. (rbb24, 4 minute read)

item.title: 🍸 KitKat club rape at sex‑positive party, rent office finds rampant usury, millionaires tripled - Berlin News Daily
item.description: Berlin’s KitKat Club has confirmed a woman was allegedly raped by a stranger at a Monday party in early September; staff called police and she was taken to hospital where a vaginal tear was recorded. The club—known for sex-positive events—said it was "deeply shaken" and will introduce publicly visible awareness teams at every party and strengthen security, while acknowledging no measure can guarantee complete safety. The woman has filed a criminal complaint. (Berliner Zeitung, 1 minute read)
